normalized_obligations.lean_statement: theorem SU1_sorted_nondec {arr : Array Int} : SortedNondecreasing arr
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: The input array (slice) is sorted in nondecreasing order (required for binary search correctness).

normalized_obligations.lean_statement: theorem SU2_some_implies_index_eq {arr : Array Int} {target : Int} {i : Nat} : binarySearch arr target = some i → i < arr.size ∧ arr[i] = target
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: If the algorithm returns Some(i), then i is within bounds and the array value at i equals the target.

normalized_obligations.lean_statement: theorem SU3_none_implies_not_mem {arr : Array Int} {target : Int} : binarySearch arr target = none → (∀ j : Nat, j < arr.size → arr[j] ≠ target)
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: If the algorithm returns None, then the target does not occur anywhere in the array.

normalized_obligations.lean_statement: theorem SU4_terminates {arr : Array Int} {target : Int} : Terminates_binarySearch arr target
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: The loop terminates on every input (the search interval shrinks each iteration).

normalized_obligations.lean_statement: theorem SU5_allows_any_matching_index {arr : Array Int} {target : Int} : ∀ i : Nat, binarySearch arr target = some i → i < arr.size ∧ arr[i] = target
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: Uniqueness is not required: any matching index is acceptable when duplicates exist.
